 {"id":"ft-9unrg","title":"frankenterm-core no-default-features fails: ars_drift tests miss DriftMonitor import","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:09:49.756811Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:09:49.756811Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-9xbxe","title":"mcp_tools test helper hits asupersync vs fastmcp_rust::asupersync version conflict","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4812\` cannot compile under
\`--features mcp\`:

\`\`\`
error[E0308]: mismatched types
    --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4812:25
     |
4812 |         McpContext::new(asupersync::Cx::for_testing(), 1)
     |         --------------- ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
     |                         expected \`fastmcp_rust::Cx\`,
     |                         found \`asupersync::Cx\`
     |
note: there are multiple different versions of crate \`asupersync\` in
      the dependency graph
\`\`\`

\`McpContext::new\` (defined in \`fastmcp_rust\`) expects
\`fastmcp_rust::Cx\` (i.e. fastmcp_rust's own vendored copy of
asupersync's Cx type), but the test helper passes the directly-imported
\`asupersync::Cx::for_testing()\` from the workspace's pinned
asupersync crate. With version drift between fastmcp_rust's vendored
asupersync (0.2.6 per the older fastmcp_rust pin) and the workspace
asupersync (0.2.9 per /Users/jemanuel/.cargo/registry path in the
error message), the two Cx types are nominally distinct even though
their fields are identical.

## Concrete failure path

1. Run \`cargo test --no-run --lib -p frankenterm-core --features mcp\`.
2. Compiler tries to build the \`#[cfg(feature = "mcp")]\` test module
   in mcp_tools.rs.
3. \`test_mcp_context()\` at line 4811-4813 fails to type-check because
   \`McpContext::new\` from fastmcp_rust expects \`fastmcp_rust::Cx\` but
   the call site passes \`asupersync::Cx\` from the workspace's
   asupersync.
4. **Entire mcp_tools test compilation blocked** — none of the ~50+
   tests in the mcp_tools test module can run, including any
   regression tests filed against mcp_tools handlers.

## Reproduction

\`\`\`
CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \\
CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \\
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p0-target \\
cargo test --no-run --lib -p frankenterm-core \\
  --no-default-features --features mcp 2>&1 | grep -B2 -A10 E0308
\`\`\`

## Severity rationale

- Blocks mcp_tools test verification entirely — any new mcp_tools test
  added under \`--features mcp\` cannot be verified locally.
- Real defect-finding work in the mcp surface (e.g. ft-tzwuw which
  found 3 missing timeout_secs guards) lands without test coverage
  because the test target won't compile.
- Not a runtime bug in production code — fastmcp_rust vs asupersync
  Cx structs are runtime-compatible; only the type system rejects
  them.

## Fix sketch

One of:
1. Bump the fastmcp_rust pin so its vendored asupersync matches the
   workspace asupersync 0.2.9 (preferred — single source of truth).
2. Replace the \`test_mcp_context\` helper to use
   \`fastmcp_rust::Cx::for_testing()\` (whatever the equivalent is in
   the fastmcp_rust API surface) instead of bridging through
   asupersync directly.
3. Add an explicit conversion (\`From<asupersync::Cx> for
   fastmcp_rust::Cx\`) in fastmcp_rust if the types are
   field-compatible.

(1) is the cleanest because it eliminates the duplicated dependency.
(2) is the smallest local change.

## Scope

Diagnostic only — fix likely lives in fastmcp_rust pin (Cargo.toml)
or the test helper. Not in scope for the agent that filed this bead
unless they have context on which fastmcp_rust SHA contains the
asupersync 0.2.9 alignment.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:52:59.938463Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:52:59.938463Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-a0up5","title":"ingest::trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes returns more than max_bytes when last char straddles the cap","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/ingest.rs:1405\` — \`trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes(text, max_bytes)\`
silently returns a string LARGER than \`max_bytes\` when the last
character would not fit. The function name and downstream callers
expect a hard \`max_bytes\` cap.

## Concrete failure path

Trace \`trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes("中", 1)\` (中 is 3 UTF-8 bytes):

1. text.len()=3, max_bytes=1. \`text.len() <= max_bytes\` (3 <= 1) is
   false; max_bytes != 0; continue.
2. start = 3.saturating_sub(1) = 2. Position 2 is in the middle of 中.
3. Snap-forward loop: is_char_boundary(2)=false → start=3.
   is_char_boundary(3)=true (text.len() is always a boundary). But the
   loop guard is \`start < text.len()\`, which is false at start=3.
   Exit with start=3.
4. \`start >= text.len()\` (3 >= 3) && max_bytes > 0 → enter the
   "fall back to last full character" branch (line 1419-1430).
5. The fallback walks backward from text.len()=3 to find the start of
   the last character (position 0 for 中), then \`saturating_sub(1)\`
   off-by-ones to position 0. (The off-by-one here is a separate issue
   from the contract violation but produces the same bytes.)
6. Returns \`text[0..]\` = "中" — **3 bytes when max_bytes was 1**.

Symmetric case: \`trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes("a中", 2)\` returns "中"
(3 bytes when 2 was requested).

## Caller impact

\`enforce_segment_size_for_persistence\` (line 1434) wraps this and
captures \`kept_bytes = truncated_content.len()\` in
\`SegmentSizeEnforcement\`. If \`kept_bytes > max_bytes\`, the
\`SegmentSizeEnforcement.kept_bytes\` field reports the violation
correctly — but the actual segment is still oversized, and:

* The truncation gap reason format string says \`"max_bytes={max}"\` —
  misleading because the kept bytes exceed it.
* Downstream consumers that allocate \`max_segment_bytes\`-sized
  buffers, set FTS5 column truncation, or enforce DB-level CHECK
  constraints on segment length get unexpectedly large input.
* Any future caller that does \`assert!(result.len() <= max_bytes)\` as
  a defensive check would fire on a config that's perfectly valid in
  the rest of the system.

## Severity rationale

- Function-contract violation: name says "to_max_bytes", behavior
  doesn't enforce that.
- Reachable when an operator configures \`max_segment_bytes\` smaller
  than the largest UTF-8 char (4 bytes) AND the segment starts with
  multi-byte content. Realistic only for misconfiguration or hostile
  input designed to exercise edge cases — but the bug exists.
- The inline comment at line 1419-1422 justifies "return the last full
  character rather than returning an empty string" — that's a
  *behavior choice*, but it violates the function's declared contract.
  Either the contract should change (function name + docs) or the
  behavior should respect the cap.

## Fix sketch

Two reasonable directions:

(A) **Strict contract** (preferred — matches function name):
  In the fallback branch, if the last character's byte length exceeds
  max_bytes, return \`String::new()\` rather than returning the
  oversize char. Callers that want "best-effort" can opt in via a
  separate API.

(B) **Document the exception**:
  Rename to \`trim_utf8_tail_with_min_one_char\` or update the doc to
  state that the result MAY exceed max_bytes when max_bytes <
  smallest character. Also update \`enforce_segment_size_for_persistence\`
  to assert / handle the over-cap case explicitly.

## Regression tests

Three tests pin the contract:
- \`trim_utf8_tail_returns_at_most_max_bytes_for_multibyte_char\` —
  \`trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes("中", 1)\` returns a string ≤ 1 byte.
- \`trim_utf8_tail_handles_2_byte_cap_with_3_byte_char\` — same for
  \`("a中", 2)\`.
- \`enforce_segment_size_respects_kept_bytes_le_max_bytes\` — the wrapper
  never produces a segment whose content exceeds max_segment_bytes.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/ingest.rs\` only. Existing tests at
lines 6051-6068 don't cover this case (max_bytes=4 with "café" tests
only the well-fitting path).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:00:25.710169Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:00:25.710169Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/ingest.rs\` only. Existing tests at
lines 6051-6068 don't cover this case (max_bytes=4 with "café" tests
only the well-fitting path).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:00:25.710169Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:00:36.330213Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-a8zzd","title":"fuzz: escape_parser_raw target accidentally reverted by misnamed disk_pressure commit","description":"# Defect

Commit `5c470432` ('fix(disk_pressure): fail closed on NaN sample instead of silently reporting Green (ft-761tz)') has a commit message describing a disk_pressure NaN fix, but `git show --numstat` reveals its actual diff is **8 files changed, 0 insertions, 90 deletions** — and every one of those deletions is a revert of `c93fa5f7` (which added the escape_parser_raw fuzz target):

```
0	5	fuzz/Cargo.toml
0	1	fuzz/corpus/escape_parser_raw/apc
0	1	fuzz/corpus/escape_parser_raw/ascii
0	1	fuzz/corpus/escape_parser_raw/csi_red
0	1	fuzz/corpus/escape_parser_raw/dcs
0	0	fuzz/corpus/escape_parser_raw/empty
0	1	fuzz/corpus/escape_parser_raw/osc_title
0	80	fuzz/fuzz_targets/escape_parser_raw.rs
```

The intended disk_pressure fix actually landed as `c5a02edb` ('fix(disk_pressure): fail closed on NaN samples in Ewma, PID, and tier classifier (ft-761tz)'). So `5c470432`'s commit message is a paste from a sibling commit, and its real effect is losing the byte-stream VT fuzzer + its 5-seed corpus that `c93fa5f7` (ft-1p752) just added.

# Why it matters

`escape_parser_raw` covers paths the existing CSI + OSC fuzzers cannot — SOS/PM/APC envelope bytes, raw DCS / Sixel sequences, nested ESC chains, the full ISO 2022 shift-state space. Those run inside `Parser::parse` on production traffic and are exactly the surface most likely to hit a termwiz regression. The seeds (apc, ascii, csi_red, dcs, empty, osc_title) were hand-selected to exercise each high-level state in the VT machine.

Without the target, ft-1p752 is reverted to 'no byte-stream coverage' even though the bead was closed.

# Fix

Restore the target + all 5 seeds + the Cargo.toml stanza exactly as landed in c93fa5f7. No other changes — this is a pure revert-of-revert.

# Repro

`git show 5c470432 --numstat` — observe 0 additions, 90 deletions. `ls fuzz/fuzz_targets/escape_parser_raw.rs` — file does not exist.

# Severity

MEDIUM — test/fuzz coverage loss, not production runtime. But the loss is silent and would have stayed silent because the bead tracking ft-1p752 is already closed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:41:55.974650Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:13.342122Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:13.341777Z","close_reason":"Restored in 3e2ae80c — pure revert-of-revert of 5c470432's accidental deletion of c93fa5f7's escape_parser_raw target, 6 seeds, and Cargo.toml stanza. Verified: cargo check --bin escape_parser_raw compiles clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-akx00","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","design":"# Execution Strategy
1. Start with inventory discipline so no suspect disappears mid-stream.
2. Attack operator-visible and machine-control surfaces first; silent stubs in control-plane APIs are the highest trust risk.
3. Replace silent pass-through behavior with either real implementations or explicit hard failures with actionable errors while work is underway.
4. Prefer shared primitives over leaf fixes. Example: alt-screen truth should come from a real pane-status pipeline, not per-call heuristics.
5. Use the no-backwards-compatibility policy aggressively: if a placeholder path should not exist, remove or collapse it instead of preserving a fake API.
6. Every completed child bead must remove the misleading placeholder signal from code and add regression tests proving the real behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"- Every verified suspect from the audit is mapped to a child bead under this epic.
- No in-scope placeholder remains in a shipping path without either a real implementation or an explicit scoped declassification.
- Comments/docs that still say "stub", "TODO", or "not implemented" on completed paths are removed or rewritten.
- Regression tests exist for each completed surface, with supported-platform behavior verified where applicable.
- A final closure comment cross-checks the audit inventory against the completed child beads with no orphaned items.
- Every sub-epic and concrete task names required unit-test coverage, required end-to-end or explicitly assigned integration coverage, and structured logging/artifact outputs before closure.
- Failure artifacts are sufficient to diagnose the issue without rerunning locally: stdout/stderr, structured logs, fixture or payload diffs, and screenshots or pixel diffs where rendering is involved.","notes":"Out-of-scope unless reclassified by evidence: test-only fake panes, intentional null-object helpers like `NoopCaptureSink`, unsupported-platform `Option::None` style shims, and vendored/upstream dependency TODOs.

This epic is intentionally structured by architectural seams rather than one bead per grep hit. The goal is to fix root causes and shared primitives, not just delete placeholder strings.

## Verification Doctrine
- Follow the shared verification contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Heavy compile/test/clippy/benchmark sweeps for this program must run via `rch exec -- ...`.
- Completion comments must record exact commands, worker/environment, and artifact locations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:27:33.773255Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:56:02.501104Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:56:02.500840Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","rendering","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"comments":[{"id":2740,"issue_id":"ft-akx00","author":"jemanuel","text":"This epic is the execution wrapper for the 2026-04-02 placeholder/stub audit. The child tracks are ordered to minimize wasted work:
- machine-control and client behavior first,
- search/connector trust next,
- core runtime and FTUI after shared state surfaces are real,
- rendering/backend parity after higher-risk control paths,
- mux/SSH operational gaps in parallel where isolated.

Future-self rule: if a new suspect appears while working these beads, either attach it to the appropriate child bead or create a new child under this epic immediately. Do not let the inventory drift back into ad hoc comments.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55Z"},{"id":3545,"issue_id":"ft-akx00","author":"jemanuel","text":"Closure ledger: All 8 children closed. ft-akx00.1 (closure ledger), ft-akx00.2 (machine-control/SDK), ft-akx00.3 (operator-support/persistence), ft-akx00.4 (connector/search), ft-akx00.5 (runtime/scrollback/recording/FTUI), ft-akx00.6 (rendering/window backends), ft-akx00.7 (tmux/mux-domain/SSH), ft-akx00.8 (shared verification contract). Full placeholder-remediation program complete.","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:55:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.1","title":"Maintain closure ledger for placeholder-remediation program","description":"# Purpose
Maintain the closure ledger for this remediation program so the audit remains complete and falsifiable.

This bead owns the suspect matrix, scope decisions, and final proof that every audited placeholder ended in a deliberate outcome. It exists to prevent the classic failure mode where implementation starts but the inventory stops being tracked.

# Deliverables
- A suspect-by-suspect ledger mapping each audited item to a child bead and final disposition.
- Explicit documentation for any item that gets reclassified as intentional or out-of-scope.
- Final cross-check before the parent epic closes.","design":"Treat the original audit as an immutable input set. For each suspect, record:
- exact file/function/branch,
- why it was considered placeholder behavior,
- which remediation bead owns it,
- what proof is required to declare it closed.

If a task discovers that the optimal fix is deletion or API collapse, record that explicitly here so future readers know it was a principled simplification rather than an omission.","acceptance_criteria":"- The ledger names every verified suspect from the 2026-04-02 audit.
- Each suspect points to exactly one owning child bead or an explicit documented reclassification.
- The final comment on this bead reports any deltas between the initial inventory and the executed work.
- The ledger records not only ownership but also the verification surface for each audited placeholder: unit tests, E2E/integration coverage, and artifact expectations.","notes":"Reopened: stalled 13-22 days with no recent activity. Prior claimant likely inactive.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SageHorse","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55.213472Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:25:16.899175Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:25:16.898914Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55.213472Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2741,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Initial ledger categories:
- Control plane/client: NTM stub response, client workspace/unilateral handling, client spawn/alt-screen, robot SDK transport.
- Operator support: chrono stub, env-bootstrap/ringlog stubs, scheduled backup compression.
- Search/connectors: transparency verification, certification probe, reranker, model2vec, fastembed sentinel behavior.
- Core runtime/UX: NullPaneScrollbackAccess, recording encodings, FTUI placeholder views, stale PaneCapabilities stub comment.
- Rendering/backends: Wayland seat+keyboard, WGL/macOS/EGL resize, bitmap/WebGPU readback, terminfo raw/crop images, background sizing, custom glyph placeholders.
- Mux/SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle, detach semantics, SFTP metadata, SSH Match Exec.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55Z"},{"id":2772,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Inventory-to-bead ledger for the 2026-04-02 placeholder audit:

Control plane / client / SDK
- `build_ntm_stub_response` fake `"status": "stub"` payload -> `ft-akx00.2.1`
- `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO + unilateral-message FIXME -> `ft-akx00.2.2`
- `ClientDomain::spawn_pane` unimplemented + `ClientPane::is_alt_screen_active()` hardcoded false -> `ft-akx00.2.3`
- generated Rust SDK transport `unimplemented!("transport not wired")` -> `ft-akx00.2.4`

Operator support / persistence utilities
- `chrono_stub_now()` -> `ft-akx00.3.1`
- `env-bootstrap` / `ringlog` stub + no-op logger -> `ft-akx00.3.2`
- scheduled backup compression placeholder -> `ft-akx00.3.3`

Connector / search
- connector transparency verification stub -> `ft-akx00.4.1`
- certification integration probe stub -> `ft-akx00.4.2`
- `CrossEncoderReranker` identity/no-op stub -> `ft-akx00.4.3`
- `Model2VecEmbedder` stub + FastEmbed sentinel constructor -> `ft-akx00.4.4`

Core runtime / FTUI / policy
- `NullPaneScrollbackAccess` placeholder -> `ft-akx00.5.1`
- `DeltaEncoding::Diff` / `Repeat` placeholders -> `ft-akx00.5.2`
- FTUI placeholder view bodies -> `ft-akx00.5.3`
- stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation in `policy.rs` -> `ft-akx00.5.4`

Rendering / windowing
- Wayland seat + keyboard TODO/unimplemented paths -> `ft-akx00.6.1`
- WGL/macOS/EGL resize + framebuffer gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.2`
- bitmap/WebGPU texture readback gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.3`
- terminfo raw/cropped image rendering gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.4`
- GUI background sizing + custom glyph placeholder rendering -> `ft-akx00.6.5`

Mux / SSH
- tmux PTY `try_wait()` + kill placeholders -> `ft-akx00.7.1`
- detach unimplemented across domains -> `ft-akx00.7.2`
- SFTP metadata mutation placeholder -> `ft-akx00.7.3`
- SSH `Match Exec` omission -> `ft-akx00.7.4`

Deliberate exclusions from this epic unless later reclassified by evidence:
- test-only fake panes and other `#[cfg(test)]` doubles
- intentional null-object helpers like `NoopCaptureSink`
- unsupported-platform no-op shims used only to represent platform absence
- vendored/upstream TODOs outside first-party source","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:33:17Z"},{"id":2798,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verification-matrix reminder:
- Shared contract bead: `ft-akx00.8`
- Every concrete child under `ft-akx00` now requires both comprehensive unit tests and a dedicated or explicitly assigned E2E/integration scenario with structured logs and artifacts.
- Completion evidence for future implementation beads should cite the exact proposed script name (or renamed final equivalent), exact commands, artifact root, and whether heavy validation ran via `rch`.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:52Z"},{"id":2813,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"SageHorse","text":"Ledger update for `ft-akx00.5.2` (closed 2026-04-03):

Suspect:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs`: dormant `DeltaEncoding::Diff` / `DeltaEncoding::Repeat` placeholder branches plus `DiffOp` placeholder type.

Disposition:
- Closed by API collapse, not by implementing fake delta machinery. Recording WAR v1 now exposes only `DeltaEncoding::Full(Vec<u8>)`, and the spec was updated to say so explicitly. This is a principled narrowing of scope, not an omission.

Proof captured:
- Code/docs updated: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_recording.rs`, `docs/recording-format-spec.md`.
- Regression: serde test now rejects the removed legacy placeholder variants.
- Targeted verification passed: `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_recording` (47 tests).
- Local formatting proof: `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_recording.rs crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_reranker.rs`.

Verification notes / non-bead blockers:
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` remains blocked by remote worker env drift (`x11-xcb.pc` missing on worker `vmi1149989`).
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` remains blocked by existing failures in `frankenterm/config/derive` and `frankenterm/dynamic/derive`.
- Fresh `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets` got past earlier test exhaustiveness drift and now fails only on unrelated bench debt in `crates/frankenterm-core/benches/input_latency_bench.rs` and `crates/frankenterm-core/benches/spike_comparison.rs`.

Cross-reference:
- Owning bead: `ft-akx00.5.2`
- Verification contract bead: `ft-akx00.8`

Follow-up for this ledger bead: accumulate the same disposition/proof block for each remaining child bead so the parent epic can be closed against a falsifiable matrix rather than scattered thread history.","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:25:13Z"},{"id":3541,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"# ft-akx00 Closure Ledger — Final Cross-Check (FoggyRobin, 2026-04-19)

## Inventory Status: 30/32 beads closed, 1 in_progress, 1 open (blocked)

### Track 2: Machine-Control, Client-Domain, SDK [CLOSED]

|------|---------|------------|-------|--------|

|------|---------|------------|-------|--------|

| ft-akx00.3.2 | env-bootstrap/ringlog stub logger | Graduated to real `env_logger::try_init()` + OnceLock idempotency | jemanuel | CLOSED |
| ft-akx00.3.3 | Scheduled backup compression placeholder | Implemented zstd compression with graceful fallback | jemanuel | CLOSED |

### Track 4: Connector Trust, Search Quality [CLOSED]
| Bead | Suspect | Disposition | Agent | Status |
|------|---------|------------|-------|--------|

### Track 5: Core Runtime, Scrollback, Recording, FTUI [CLOSED]
| Bead | Suspect | Disposition | Agent | Status |
|------|---------|------------|-------|--------|
| ft-akx00.5.1 | `NullPaneScrollbackAccess` in production | Replaced with `SnapshotPaneScrollbackAccess`; Null relegated to test-only | FoggyLark | CLOSED |
| ft-akx00.5.2 | `DeltaEncoding::Diff/Repeat` dormant branches | API collapse — narrowed to `DeltaEncoding::Full` only | SageHorse | CLOSED |
| ft-akx00.5.3 | FTUI placeholder view bodies | Live state-backed surfaces | jemanuel | CLOSED |

| ft-akx00.5 | Epic rollup | All 4 children closed | jemanuel | CLOSED |

### Track 6: Rendering, Window Backends [4/5 closed, 1 in_progress]
| Bead | Suspect | Disposition | Agent | Status |
|------|---------|------------|-------|--------|
| ft-akx00.6.1 | Wayland seat/keyboard `todo!()` paths | Real seat teardown + keyboard leave handling | SilentSpire/BrightCrane | CLOSED |
| ft-akx00.6.2 | WGL/macOS/EGL resize/framebuffer gaps | Confirmed implemented; backing-size normalization added | IndigoFox/BrightCrane | CLOSED |
| ft-akx00.6.3 | Bitmap/WebGPU texture readback `unimplemented!()` | Implementation in progress | jemanuel | IN_PROGRESS |
| ft-akx00.6.4 | Terminfo raw/cropped image rendering gaps | Implemented iTerm2 image rendering for raw/cropped/animated RGBA | IndigoFox | CLOSED |
| ft-akx00.6.5 | GUI background-sizing + custom glyph placeholders | Real `Contain`/`Cover` + scalable diagonal hatches | BrightCrane | CLOSED |
| ft-akx00.6 | Epic rollup | Blocked by ft-akx00.6.3 | — | OPEN |

### Track 7: Tmux, Mux-Domain, SSH [CLOSED]
| Bead | Suspect | Disposition | Agent | Status |
|------|---------|------------|-------|--------|

### Track 8: Shared Verification Contract [CLOSED]

|------|---------|------------|-------|--------|
| ft-akx00.8 | Verification/E2E/artifact contract | `docs/test-logging-contract.md` + `docs/e2e-harness-spec.md` | SunnyTiger/IndigoFox | CLOSED |

## Deliberate Exclusions (unchanged from initial audit)
- Test-only fake panes and `#[cfg(test)]` doubles
- Intentional null-object helpers (e.g., `NoopCaptureSink`)
- Unsupported-platform no-op shims representing platform absence
- Vendored/upstream TODOs outside first-party source

## Gap Analysis
**Only remaining gap**: ft-akx00.6.3 (texture readback across bitmap/WebGPU) — currently in_progress, assigned to jemanuel. Once this closes, ft-akx00.6 epic and then the parent ft-akx00 epic can close.

**No orphaned items**: Every suspect from the 2026-04-02 audit maps to exactly one child bead. No suspects were lost or reclassified without documentation.

**Infrastructure blockers noted but not code-level**: Several rch workers lack platform toolchains (wayland-client.pc, x11.pc, Windows/macOS cross-CC). These limit CI verification breadth but do not indicate missing code.","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:25:08Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-s9oci","title":"mux/domain follow-ups: blocking spawn_command in async, silent write! errors","description":"Deferred during /multi-pass-bug-hunting audit shipped as dcaf60ca. Three pass-1/2 findings were clean in domain.rs, but two design-level issues remain as follow-up work:

1. Blocking spawn inside async fn. LocalDomain::spawn_pane (domain.rs:667) calls pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd) which on Unix is a synchronous fork+exec. If the child binary or an interposed fork hook hangs, the async task's executor thread is blocked indefinitely. Fix direction: route the spawn through tokio::task::spawn_blocking (or the asupersync equivalent) and await the join handle. Scope: also audit pair.master.take_writer() on the same path.

2. Silently swallowed write! errors. LocalDomain::spawn_pane on the child-spawn-failure branch (domain.rs:693) renders the error into the new pane via write!(writer, "{err:#}").ok(). If the write itself fails the user sees a blank pane with no indication of the upstream spawn failure. Fix direction: log the nested error via tracing::warn! with both the original spawn err and the write err so the failure is at least visible in logs.

Auditor: pane 6 (2026-04-20). Audit context: 91% context budget, committed primary fix + this bead for the residual scope before /clear.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:59:03.628753Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:59:35.409355Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:59:35.409051Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 5c8c417c. Item 1: spawn_command now runs via promise::spawn::spawn_into_new_thread so fork+exec no longer blocks the async executor thread. Item 2: silent write! errors were already fixed earlier in f6019f0f (replaced .ok() with and_then(flush)+tracing::warn! on both err paths).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async-safety","domain","follow-up","mux"]}
 {"id":"ft-sl1xk","title":"shell_escape must not turn empty restore cwd into bare cd","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:31:45.320983Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:39:05.107651Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:39:05.107317Z","close_reason":"already landed in 3b525792","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-swa8k","title":"[SEV] workflows/runner.rs orphan-cx fallbacks break ambient-cx inheritance in 3 sites","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs lines 729, 1498, 2021 all use the stale fallback pattern:

  let request_cx = crate::cx::for_request();
  let injector_cx = cx.unwrap_or(&request_cx);

When the caller passes cx=None but an ambient Cx is present (Cx::current() is Some), this falls back to a brand-new for_request() cx instead of inheriting the ambient one. That is the exact bug commit 02a599a9 (refactor(workflows): inherit ambient cx in pane injection wrappers) fixed for the four WorkflowContext::send_* entry points by switching to:

  let cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);

Concrete consequences:
- Line 729 (run_workflow_inner replay_capture setup): set_decision_capture runs under an orphan cx, so a parent cancellation cannot propagate through the capture adapter.
- Line 1498 (run_workflow_inner step loop send_text branch): policy-gated send_text for legacy non-cx callers injects under an orphan cx; parent cancellation does not abort the injection or its audit write.
- Line 2021 (pub async fn run event loop child spawn): runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx is invoked with a fresh cx, orphaning the spawned workflow from the runtime's ambient cx — cancelling the ambient cx does NOT cancel the child workflow.

Real non-test callers hit these paths: mcp_tools.rs:2145 calls runner.run_workflow(...) from an MCP handler (MCP handlers run under an ambient request cx), so MCP-initiated workflows run to completion even if the MCP request is cancelled. The run() event loop at line 1955 is the non-cx sibling of run_with_cx (line 2154) and can be invoked from inside a with_cx block — children spawned there escape the ambient scope.

Fix: replace each 'let request_cx = crate::cx::for_request();' with the ambient-inheriting pattern 'let request_cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);'. No behaviour change when no ambient cx is present; closes the ambient-inheritance gap for every caller that already has one.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T22:07:15.266712Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T22:12:37.792231Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T22:12:37.792041Z","close_reason":"Completed: replaced 3 stale for_request() fallbacks with Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request) in runner.rs (replay_capture setup, step-loop send_text, run() event-loop child spawn). cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib passes clean. Commit 09014aae.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-szuzd","title":"mcp_tools: schema-declared numeric maxima on wa.cass_* and wa.wait_for/wa.send are not enforced by handlers","description":"Several wa.* MCP tools declare numeric bounds in their input_schema but only enforce the LOWER bound at runtime. The UPPER bound is silently ignored because serde_json doesn't honour JSON-Schema's maximum (that's exactly why ft-tzwuw added the explicit timeout_secs == 0 check). Same shape applies to the maximum side:

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:526 wa.cass_search declares 'timeout_secs: {minimum: 1, maximum: 600}' but only checks == 0 at :572. A client sending timeout_secs: 3600 is accepted; cass_client_with_timeout(3600) builds a 1-hour timeout; cass can block the mcp server for up to an hour.
- Same file wa.cass_search declares 'limit: {minimum: 0, maximum: 1000}' with no runtime bound at all — a client sending limit: u64::MAX stages that into CassSearchOptions and the downstream cass call.
- wa.cass_view (:657), wa.cass_expand (:763), wa.wait_for (:1202-area), wa.send (:1926-area) repeat the same shape for timeout_secs.

wa.events at :1725-1749 shows the gold-standard pattern (LIMIT_MIN/LIMIT_MAX constants + matching error message). Fix: replicate that pattern for every tool whose schema declares a minimum/maximum that the handler doesn't already enforce. Scope this bead to wa.cass_search first (both bounds); sibling tools follow-on with pointers to this bead.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:57:22.700960Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:57:22.700960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

The UPPER bound is silently ignored because serde_json doesn't honour JSON-Schema's maximum (that's exactly why ft-tzwuw added the explicit timeout_secs == 0 check). Same shape applies to the maximum side:

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:526 wa.cass_search declares 'timeout_secs: {minimum: 1, maximum: 600}' but only checks == 0 at :572. A client sending timeout_secs: 3600 is accepted; cass_client_with_timeout(3600) builds a 1-hour timeout; cass can block the mcp server for up to an hour.
- Same file wa.cass_search declares 'limit: {minimum: 0, maximum: 1000}' with no runtime bound at all — a client sending limit: u64::MAX stages that into CassSearchOptions and the downstream cass call.
- wa.cass_view (:657), wa.cass_expand (:763), wa.wait_for (:1202-area), wa.send (:1926-area) repeat the same shape for timeout_secs.

wa.events at :1725-1749 shows the gold-standard pattern (LIMIT_MIN/LIMIT_MAX constants + matching error message). Fix: replicate that pattern for every tool whose schema declares a minimum/maximum that the handler doesn't already enforce. Scope this bead to wa.cass_search first (both bounds); sibling tools follow-on with pointers to this bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:57:22.700960Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:00:36.248624Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:00:36.248425Z","close_reason":"Landed in 4dc2d752 — 132 lines of mcp_tools.rs changes bundled with another agent's session_restore/snapshot_engine ft-rfpk6 fix (concurrent-agent omnibus commit). The bundled changes are exactly the wa.cass_search timeout_secs + limit upper-bound guards + three regression tests (ft_tzwuw_ca_search_rejects_zero_timeout_secs updated to range form, ft_szuzd_ca_search_rejects_above_max_timeout_secs, ft_szuzd_ca_search_rejects_above_max_limit). Parallel gaps in wa.cass_view/wa.cass_expand/wa.wait_for/wa.send remain — follow-on sweeps can mirror this same pattern.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-t0gd0","title":"retry: typed RuntimeOperation cancellation remains retryable and trips circuit breaker","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:31:31.764204Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:18.840048Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:18.839178Z","close_reason":"Landed in d2c0dac7 (fix(retry): treat RuntimeOperation::Cancelled the same as Error::Cancelled)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-t62hq","title":"[SEV] mcp wa.wait_for + wa.send accept timeout_secs=0 despite schema minimum:1 — silent zero-duration wait","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs WaWaitForTool (line 1085) and WaSendTool (line 1771) advertise input_schema with 'timeout_secs: { type: integer, minimum: 1, default: 30 }' (lines 1095 and 1783). WaitForParams.timeout_secs and SendParams.timeout_secs are both u64 in mcp_types.rs (lines 232, 241), which serde_json::from_value accepts 0 for — serde does not enforce JSON-Schema min/max/default. No post-parse bounds check exists at either call site.

Concrete defect: a client sending { pane_id: 1, pattern: 'OK', timeout_secs: 0 } to wa.wait_for hits line 1166 'let timeout = std::time::Duration::from_secs(timeout_secs);' with timeout = 0. The wait loop times out immediately; the tool reports matched: false, polls: 0, elapsed_ms: 0 even if the pattern would have appeared in the pane microseconds later. Same gap in wa.send at line 1928. Clients that type-coerce (e.g., JS Math.floor(0.5) = 0, CLI timeout=0 as 'no timeout') land on this silently.

Precedent: the wa.events tool at line 1638 already enforces its advertised 'limit: minimum:1, maximum:1000' bounds post-parse with a well-documented comment stating 'many MCP clients don't validate tool inputs against the schema before sending'. That comment applies equally to timeout_secs.

Fix: add the same post-parse validation for timeout_secs at both call sites, returning MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS with a message pointing at the schema's minimum. Pattern matches the wa.events enforcement block exactly.

Also note (tracked separately if filed): wa.cass_search, wa.cass_view, wa.cass_status (lines 511, 621, 711) advertise 'timeout_secs: minimum:1, maximum:600' with the same Rust u64 -> schema mismatch. They pass params.timeout_secs into cass_client_with_timeout (line 278) which accepts any u64. This bead scopes to wa.wait_for + wa.send only to keep the fix focused.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:25:59.494378Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:29:38.622911Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:29:38.622531Z","close_reason":"Completed: added post-parse timeout_secs>=1 validation at WaWaitForTool.call and WaSendTool.call matching the wa.events limit-enforcement pattern (line 1638). Clients sending timeout_secs=0 now receive MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS with a schema-referencing hint instead of a silent zero-duration no-op. cass tools (lines 511/621/711) have the same gap, scoped out. Commit 8dc7dbde.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-t995m","title":"escape-parser: tmux_cc feature build broken (130 compile errors) — proptest macro expansion E0277 on Vec<usize>/String strategies","description":"cargo test -p frankenterm-escape-parser --lib --features tmux_cc fails with 130 compile errors. All from the existing proptest block at tmux_cc/mod.rs:1397-1409 (parser_events_are_chunk_boundary_invariant, added by 3abc11d2). Error shape: E0277 'the size for values of type [usize] cannot be known at compilation time' inside proptest::sugar::NamedArguments<N, V> — the macro is trying to use slice-unsized V where it should be Vec-sized. Also 'str' / '[usize]' sized errors with the same root cause. Looks like a proptest 1.11 macro-expansion breakage or a version mismatch between the workspace pin (1.11) and the crate's dev-dep pin (1.8). The tmux_cc feature tests never run in CI when this is broken — my ft-kfy99 fix is latent until this unblocks. Scope: this bead only tracks the test-build breakage; the underlying logic change in ft-kfy99 is unaffected.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:05.738611Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:05.738611Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
